name: CrateDB
description: Distributed SQL database for real-time analytics, optimized for time-series, full-text search, and machine data

hosting-options.comment: CrateDB Cloud available on AWS, Azure, and GCP. Also available via AWS and Azure Marketplaces.

initial-release.comment: CrateDB was first released in 2014 by Crate.io.

data-model.comment: Supports relational tables, JSON documents, time-series data, geospatial, full-text, and vector data within a single system.

topology.comment: Shared-nothing distributed architecture. Every node is equal in terms of functionality.

sharding.comment: Data is automatically distributed across nodes through auto-sharding and auto-rebalancing.

replication-factor.comment: Default is 1 replica per shard. Configurable per table.

multi-region.comment: Supports nodes across multiple zones, data centers, or geographical regions.

cap-position.comment: Eventual consistency is the trade-off CrateDB makes for high availability. Prioritizes availability over consistency.

consistency-model.comment: Eventual consistency across replicas with MVCC. Row-level atomicity and durability. Get operations see immediate writes via translog.

acid-transactions.comment: CrateDB does not support ACID transactions. Offers row-level atomicity and optimistic concurrency control via versioning.

transaction-scope.comment: Operations are atomic at the row level only. No multi-statement transaction support.

isolation-levels.comment: CrateDB does not support traditional isolation levels as it doesn't have multi-statement transactions.

distributed-transactions.comment: No distributed transaction coordination. Uses optimistic concurrency control with versioning instead.

sql-compatibility.comment: PostgreSQL wire protocol compatible. Standard PostgreSQL drivers work, but autocommit should be enabled as transactions aren't supported.

secondary-indexes.comment: Every field is automatically indexed. Built on Lucene.

joins.comment: Supports distributed JOINs, sub-selects, and ad-hoc queries across the cluster.

full-text-search.comment: Native full-text search powered by Apache Lucene.

max-cluster-size.comment: No specific maximum cluster size documented. CrateDB is designed to scale horizontally.

write-throughput.comment: No independent benchmark figures available. Vendor claims real-time ingestion at scale.

read-latency-p99.comment: No independent benchmark figures available. Designed for in-memory speed analytics.

auto-scaling.comment: Automatic rebalancing when nodes are added/removed. CrateDB Cloud supports elastic scaling.

hot-spot-handling.comment: Automatic rebalancing of data across nodes.

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Rolling upgrades supported between consecutive feature versions with zero downtime.

online-schema-changes.comment: Dynamic schema evolution. New fields can be added automatically without downtime or migrations.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Snapshots represent state at snapshot time. No continuous point-in-time recovery to arbitrary timestamps.

backup-strategy.comment: Snapshot-based backups with incremental support. Can store on S3, Azure Blob, or local filesystem.

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official Kubernetes operator with Helm chart. Supports safe scaling, rolling upgrades, and SSL.

rust-driver.comment: Uses Rust PostgreSQL drivers via PostgreSQL wire protocol compatibility.

go-driver.comment: Available via ecosystem using PostgreSQL wire protocol.

java-driver.comment: Official JDBC driver based on PostgreSQL JDBC. Pure Java, Type 4 driver.

dotnet-driver.comment: Available via ecosystem using PostgreSQL wire protocol.

python-driver.comment: Official crate client library implementing Python DB API 2.0. Also supports SQLAlchemy.

ruby-driver.comment: Uses Ruby PostgreSQL drivers via PostgreSQL wire protocol compatibility.

c-cpp-driver.comment: Uses C/C++ PostgreSQL drivers via PostgreSQL wire protocol compatibility.

orm-support.comment: SQLAlchemy support for Python. Hibernate works via JDBC. Frameworks using PostgreSQL drivers generally compatible.

free-tier.comment: CrateDB Cloud offers a forever-free tier for development and testing.

pricing-model.comment: Pricing based on cluster configuration. Plans range from ~$51/month to $1400+/month based on resources.

cost-estimate-small.comment: Based on CrateDB Cloud pricing. CR1 ~$187/month per node, CR2 ~$437/month per node.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Unable to retrieve exact count from Stack Overflow.

change-data-capture.comment: No built-in CDC. Supports ingesting CDC from other databases via Debezium and Kafka, but not as a source.

watch-subscribe.comment: No native push-based subscription mechanism for data changes.

triggers.comment: CrateDB does not support database-side triggers.

event-streaming.comment: Integrates with Kafka via Debezium/StreamSets. Supports AWS DMS with Kinesis for data ingestion. Acts as sink, not source.
